title: fix: seekToRange silently dropped when streaming poll in-flight
description:
Race condition in use-tick-poller.ts: seekToRange returns early if fetchingRef.current is true (set by streaming fetchForward poll). Since fetchForward runs every 200ms, there's a high chance seekToRange is silently dropped when user clicks Apply in the WindowRangeTrimmer.

Consequence: user enters paused mode (goToTick fires), manualWindowActive is set (streaming stops), but the range fetch never happens. User is stuck viewing stale data.

Fix approach: seekToRange must preempt in-flight streaming polls. Use a request generation counter: seekToRange increments a seekGenRef and proceeds regardless of fetchingRef. fetchForward's .then() checks if seekGenRef changed during flight and discards its result if so. seekToRange should also reset fetchingRef before starting its own fetch.

Files: packages/studio-ui/src/hooks/use-tick-poller.ts (primary), possibly packages/react/src/hooks/useInspector.ts and BehaviourTreeDebugger.tsx for edge cases.
closeReason: Fixed seekToRange race condition: added seekGenRef generation counter so seekToRange preempts in-flight streaming polls. fetchForward discards stale results in .then() and .catch() when seekGen changes.

title: fix: off-by-one in seekToRange — fromTickId excluded
description:
When user selects window starting at tick 132, the first tick returned is 133. The range query uses inclusive bounds (>=, <=) on the server, so the issue is likely in how fromTickId is computed or passed from the WindowRangeTrimmer. Check fracToTick rounding, the limit calculation (toTickId - fromTickId + 1 vs off-by-one), and the server-side findRange query.

Files: packages/react/src/components/panels/WindowRangeTrimmer.tsx (fracToTick), packages/studio-ui/src/hooks/use-tick-poller.ts (seekToRange limit), packages/studio-server/src/infra/knex/tick-repository.ts (findRange).
closeReason: Merged branch overstory/builder-obo-fix/behavior-tree-ist-f09c

title: feat: tick store size should match selected window size
description:
When user selects a window via WindowRangeTrimmer, the inspector maxTicks should equal the window size (toTickId - fromTickId + 1), not the default ringBufferSize (500). Currently the inspector always uses ringBufferSize=500, so if the user selects a window of 1000 ticks, only 500 survive ingestion. If they select 100, the store is unnecessarily large.

The fix should propagate the selected window size from onFetchTickRange through to the inspector's maxTicks. This likely means:
1. use-studio-controls.ts: expose the active window size (or null when streaming)
2. BehaviourTreeDebugger: when a manual window is active, override inspectorOptions.maxTicks with the window size
3. Alternatively, compute dynamically: when seekToRange is called with (from, to), set a windowMaxTicks = to - from + 1 and expose it alongside isLoadingWindow

Files: packages/studio-ui/src/hooks/use-tick-poller.ts, packages/studio-ui/src/use-studio-controls.ts, packages/react/src/BehaviourTreeDebugger.tsx, packages/studio-ui/src/App.tsx
closeReason: Added windowMaxTicks to StudioControls; propagated from onFetchTickRange through to App.tsx inspectorOptions.maxTicks

title: refactor: simplify time travel window selection — double-fetch race
description:
ROOT CAUSE: handleTrimmerApply in TimelinePanel.tsx calls both onSelectRange(from,to) (→ seekToRange, correct query) and controls.goToTick(from). goToTick checks storedIds which still has the OLD window (seekToRange is async), so tick 'from' is not found → onNeedTick fires → onFetchTicksAround fires a SECOND seekToRange with a much larger window. The second seekToRange bumps seekGenRef, invalidating the first correct response. UI gets the wrong window.

IMMEDIATE FIX: handleTrimmerApply should NOT call goToTick(from) after onSelectRange. Instead, after seekToRange resolves and ticks are loaded, the UI should auto-navigate to the first tick of the new window. The simplest approach: remove goToTick(from) from handleTrimmerApply, and when seekToRange completes (isLoadingWindow transitions false→true→false), auto-set frozenTickId to the oldest tick in the new window.

BROADER CLEANUP: The interaction between goToTick (onNeedTick side-effect), onFetchTicksAround, onFetchTickRange, and seekToRange is unnecessarily layered. Simplify: when a range is explicitly selected, that should be the ONLY fetch. goToTick should only navigate within already-loaded ticks, never trigger fetches during a pending seekToRange.

FILES TO MODIFY:
- packages/react/src/components/panels/TimelinePanel.tsx (handleTrimmerApply — remove goToTick call)
- packages/react/src/BehaviourTreeDebugger.tsx (auto-navigate to first tick after window load completes)
- packages/react/src/hooks/useTimeTravelControls.ts (goToTick should not fire onNeedTick when a seek is in-flight, or expose a navigateOnly variant)
- packages/studio-ui/src/use-studio-controls.ts (review onFetchTicksAround interaction)

ACCEPTANCE: yarn typecheck && yarn lint && yarn test. Selecting a window range via trimmer should fire exactly ONE ticks.range query. After the window loads, the scrubber should automatically navigate to the first tick of the selected range.
closeReason: Merged branch overstory/builder-double-fetch/behavior-tree-ist-41d7

title: fix: start live polling from latest ticks, not tick 0
description:
When entering live mode, the poller starts with afterTickId=0, fetching from the very beginning. Instead it should:
1. Query ticks.bounds to get maxTickId
2. Fetch the last ringBufferSize ticks (from maxTickId - ringBufferSize to maxTickId) as the initial window
3. Then start polling forward from maxTickId

This gives the user an immediate view of recent activity instead of watching ticks load from the start.

FILES: packages/studio-ui/src/hooks/use-tick-poller.ts (fetchForward initial behavior, streaming mode setup)

Incremental diff-ingestion only applies to live ring buffer updates.

title: TT window bugs round 2: wrong ticks on first apply + selector overflow (with Playwright QA)

title: WHI-16: implement remote REPL terminal plugin (core + server + UI)
description:
Implement the remote REPL terminal as a plugin for the studio agent. This is a large feature spanning core, server, and UI.

PHASE 1 — Core plugin system:
- Add MessageType.PluginMessage = 7 to protocol.ts
- Plugin envelope: { t: 7, pluginId: string, correlationId: string, payload: unknown }
- StudioAgent.registerPlugin(plugin) — generic interface, agent stays fully agnostic to plugin details
- Plugin class instantiated with its own config (keys, timeouts, etc.), then registered

PHASE 2 — REPL plugin (agent-side):
- new Function() eval with console injection (Frostmod pattern)
- Declaration rewriting for persistence (let x = 1 → globalThis.x = 1)
- Expression detection (isProbablyExpression)
- Console capture (monkey-patch during eval)
- 15s timeout
- Result serialization (toDisplayString)
- Property enumeration for tab-completion (walk prototype chain)
- NaCl encryption (tweetnacl + @noble/hashes): box key exchange, secretbox per-message, HKDF session keys

PHASE 3 — Server:
- Plugin message handler routing by pluginId
- REPL broker extending AbstractCommandBroker pattern
- tRPC routes for eval + completions

PHASE 4 — UI:
- xterm.js terminal component
- Ubuntu terminal theme
- Enter to send, Shift+Enter for newline
- Tab-completion round-trip
- Command history (up/down arrows)
- Console output rendering (batched, not streaming)
- Key management: Generate Keypair button, store private key in localStorage, show public key for copy
- Panel placement: bottom panel or canvas overlay

Reference: git submodule references/Frostmod (run git submodule update --init). Direct port patterns from repl-service.ts, crypto.ts, repl-waiter.ts, repl-bridge-handler.ts.

Research comments on WHI-16 cover all design decisions. No open questions.

title: Fix REPL terminal keyboard input issues (dead keys, ghost characters)
description:

Instead of reaching xterm.js, these keystrokes are captured by an input field at the top of the canvas (likely the node search/filter input in BehaviourTreeDebugger). The REPL tab needs to ensure no other input steals focus or intercepts key events when the terminal is active.

2. **Ghost characters (000000, )))))) appearing)**: Repeated zero or paren characters appear in the terminal output without user input. Likely caused by xterm.js receiving raw keyCode/charCode events that get mis-decoded, or a key event listener conflict between the canvas and the terminal.

- Check if BehaviourTreeDebugger or canvas has a hidden search/filter input that captures keydown events globally
- Check xterm.js terminal focus management when switching to REPL tab
- Check for event listeners (keydown/keypress) on document/window that may conflict with xterm.js
- Verify xterm.js onData/onKey handlers in ReplTerminal.tsx
- Playwright testing allowed for reproducing and verifying fixes

## Acceptance criteria
- All keyboard characters typeable on international keyboard layout (backtick, quotes, accented chars)
- No ghost characters appearing in terminal
- Terminal properly captures all keyboard input when REPL tab is active
- No regression in canvas keyboard shortcuts when on debugger tab

title: Fix REPL multi-line output indentation (\n → \r\n)
description:
Object output like {a:1, b:2} has staircase indentation because convertEol was removed.

Root cause: result.text from JSON.stringify contains 
 but the terminal needs 
. The writeln() function adds 
 at the end but embedded newlines within the text are just 
, so each line starts at the current cursor column instead of column 0.

Fix in packages/studio-ui/src/repl/ReplTerminal.tsx printResult() function (~line 39-50): replace 
 with 
 in result.text and consoleOutput lines before writing to terminal.

Simple approach: in printResult, do text.replace(/
/g, '
') on result.text and each consoleOutput line before passing to writeln().

title: Fix REPL multi-line input redraw explosion
description:
Multi-line input via Shift+Enter is completely broken. Each keystroke triggers redrawInputLine() which:
1. Does \r\x1b[K — only clears the CURRENT terminal line
2. Writes the full multi-line buffer (with \n chars from Shift+Enter)
3. Each subsequent line staircases because bare \n doesn't return to column 0
4. Previous multi-line renders aren't cleared, so content accumulates

The redrawInputLine function was designed for single-line input only. For multi-line buffers it needs to:
- Track how many terminal lines the previous render occupied (linesRenderedRef)
- Move cursor up that many lines before clearing
- Clear ALL lines of the previous render (loop: clear line + move down, then move back up)
- Then write the new content with \r\n for embedded newlines
- Update linesRenderedRef with new line count

Also the syntax highlighting change made onData always use full redraw (not just append at end), which means even single characters trigger the broken multi-line redraw path.

File: packages/studio-ui/src/repl/ReplTerminal.tsx — redrawInputLine function and its callers

title: Replace custom REPL input handling with local-echo library
description:
Replace the hand-rolled onData/onKey input handling in ReplTerminal.tsx with a battle-tested local-echo library for xterm.js.

## Background
We've had 6+ rounds of bugs with our custom terminal input handling (ghost chars, arrow keys, multi-line redraw, selection offset, completions navigation). The root cause is that xterm.js is a terminal emulator, not a line editor — every REPL needs a local-echo layer.

## Libraries to evaluate (pick best fit)
- wavesoft/local-echo (159 stars, original) — multi-line, history, tab completion
- SchokkerIT/xterm-local-echo — fork with multi-line continuation
- pjsg/xterm-local-echo — another maintained fork
- strtok/xterm-readline — readline-like addon

## Approach
1. Evaluate which library best fits (consider: xterm.js v5 compat, ESM support, multi-line editing, tab completion hooks, actively maintained)
2. May revert/remove the current custom input handling entirely
3. Integrate the chosen library, wiring up:
   - Our doEval() for Enter/submit
   - Our doCompletions() for Tab
   - Syntax highlighting (may need to hook into the library's render)
   - History
   - Multi-line input via Shift+Enter or auto-continuation
4. Keep: printResult(), KeyManagement panel, useRepl hook, ANSI color helpers
5. Remove: custom onData/onKey handlers, cursorPosRef, linesRenderedRef, manual redraw logic

## Acceptance
- All current REPL features work (eval, completions, history, multi-line)
- No ghost characters, no selection offset, no staircase rendering
- Arrow keys work for caret movement and history
- Syntax highlighting preserved if possible
- yarn check passes
closeReason: Merged - replaced custom input with xterm-readline

title: Fix Tab completions broken after xterm-readline migration
description:
Tab completions stopped working after migrating to xterm-readline. The handleTab() function in ReplTerminal.tsx accesses readline's private state via cast (RlInternal). 

Likely issues:
1. The 'state' property is TypeScript private but accessible at JS runtime — verify this actually works
2. state.buffer() or state.update() may have different signatures in the installed version
3. The async gap in handleTab (await sendCompletions) may cause state to be invalidated
4. The attachCustomKeyEventHandler returning false for Tab may prevent readline from processing it but may also prevent the key event from reaching our handler properly

Debug approach: add console.log in handleTab to check if rlInternal.state exists, what buffer() returns, etc. Then fix accordingly.

Also: xterm-readline has its own completion API — check if we should use setCheckHandler or some built-in completion mechanism instead of hacking private internals.

title: Rearchitect REPL to true E2E encryption — strip server crypto
description:
MAJOR REFACTOR: Convert REPL from server-mediated encryption to true end-to-end encryption between browser and agent. Server becomes a blind relay.

WHAT TO CHANGE:
1. SERVER (repl-broker.ts): Remove serverSecretKey, remove all encrypt/decrypt, remove session key derivation. Server only relays opaque encrypted blobs between UI and agent. handleAgentMessage just resolves the pending promise with the raw encrypted payload.
2. UI (use-repl.ts): Complete the existing dead crypto code. UI encrypts eval/completions payloads before sending via tRPC. UI decrypts agent responses. Handshake happens between UI and agent (server relays the headerToken).
3. tRPC SCHEMA (repl.ts handler): Accept encryptedPayload field. Pass it through to repl-broker without inspection.
4. AGENT (studio-plugins REPL plugin): Agent uses UI's public key for handshake, not server's. Agent encrypts/decrypts directly with UI's session keys.
5. HANDSHAKE: UI creates headerToken sealed to agent's public key. Server relays it. Agent opens it and derives session keys. Agent sends its headerToken back (relayed by server). UI opens it and derives matching session keys.
6. Remove vestigial core/repl-crypto.ts.
7. Fix getRandomBytes() Math.random() fallback — throw instead.
8. Fix misleading comment in repl-types.ts:52.

Files affected: repl-broker.ts, use-repl.ts, repl.ts (tRPC handler), studio-plugins/repl-plugin, repl-types.ts, repl-crypto.ts
closeReason: Merged all 3 builders. E2E REPL encryption complete: server stripped of crypto (blind relay), ReplClient class extracted in studio-plugins, use-repl.ts wired with auto-handshake.

title: Security audit of REPL E2E encryption system
description: Audit the full REPL E2E encryption architecture for security vulnerabilities. Review: (1) handshake protocol — is the ephemeral keypair + sessionSeed sealing to UI pubkey sound? (5) demo keypair — risks of hardcoded keys shipping in production, (6) server blind relay — can the server tamper with or replay messages even without decrypting? (7) key storage in localStorage — XSS implications, (8) overall protocol — compare against established patterns (Signal, Noise, etc), identify any novel/non-standard choices that could be weak. title: WHI-24: Implement MCP server for EVAL with E2E encryption
description:
Implement packages/studio-mcp — an MCP server that connects to studio-server via tRPC HTTP and provides eval/completions access to remote agents with full E2E encryption via ReplClient.

Owner answers to open questions:
Q1 (Key distribution): Shared key model — UI and MCP share the same private key. The key is not owned by the UI, it's just generated/set there for convenience.
Q2 (Stale sessions): Lazy re-handshake on decryption failure.
Q3 (Multi-key agents): Fail loudly — clear error 'Agent X is not configured with this MCP public key'.
Q4 (Timeouts): Use server defaults, allow override via tool input.
Q5 (Package location): packages/studio-mcp (option a — full monorepo package).
Q6 (MCP SDK): Standard @modelcontextprotocol/sdk ^1.17.x with stdio transport.
Q7 (Auth): No auth for now — private key serves as authentication to REPL.
Q8 (list_agents REPL readiness): Yes, check handshake token availability.
Q9 (vs Frostmod): Our implementation is more solid with typed tRPC, ReplClient, etc.

Architecture:
- packages/studio-mcp/ with src/index.ts, config.ts, session-manager.ts, tools.ts
- 3 MCP tools: list_agents, eval, completions (+ get_public_key)
- Typed tRPC client with httpLink (not httpBatchLink)
- ReplClient from @bt-studio/studio-plugins for all crypto
- Config via env: BT_STUDIO_URL, BT_STUDIO_PRIVATE_KEY (hex or base64url)
- Auto-select single online agent, lazy re-handshake on failure
- No server-side changes needed

See research comments on Linear WHI-24 for full architecture proposal and scout reports.

title: Monitor UI: filter own evals + narrow to attached agent
description:
Two client-side filtering improvements for the REPL monitor view:

1. SELF-EVAL FILTERING: The monitor should NOT display evals that the UI itself initiated. The useReplMonitor hook should compare incoming encryptedRequest payloads against requests sent by useRepl. If it matches a request the UI sent, skip rendering it in the monitor. This way the monitor only shows external activity (MCP, other clients).

2. AGENT NARROWING: The monitor should only display evals and responses for the currently attached agent (the selected clientId/sessionId). Filter incoming repl.activity WS events by matching against the currently selected agent in the UI.

Both filters are purely client-side in useReplMonitor — no server or protocol changes needed.
closeReason: Monitor UI filters: self-eval filtering via encrypted payload Set tracking, agent narrowing already in place. Merged.
